mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate near the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and reduces the opportunity cost of holding stocks relative to cash, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1%.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS remains under 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 6-month window signals improving labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales growth and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly reinforces GDP growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory consumption spikes.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show 20-day forward return hit rate below 50% or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or single-series models outperform the joint model.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle industrial recovery, rising industrial production signals restocking and capacity utilization gains that translate into higher forward corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to weaken or reverse in late-cycle regimes where INDPRO gains signal overheating and margin compression.
regime_hint: early-cycle industrial recovery following recession trough
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trend fails to predict positive 60-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods classified as early-cycle recovery based on GDPC1 troughs, or effect direction flips sign versus late-cycle sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payroll employment growth reflects real-time labor demand strength that is quickly priced into equities within a week, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates above a 2.5% year-over-year threshold, markets anticipate future monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing recovery regime where housing starts are rising from depressed post-recession levels, continued HOUST growth signals durable consumer balance-sheet repair and construction-sector employment gains that support equity valuations over a quarterly horizon; in a mature or overheated housing regime, the same HOUST growth signals late-cycle exuberance and predicts weaker or negative forward returns.
regime_hint: post-recession housing recovery with FEDFUNDS near zero
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict positive 60-day SPX forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods with FEDFUNDS below 1%, or the direction of the effect matches late-cycle high-rate regimes rather than diverging from them.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a still-accommodative federal funds rate signals that the economy can absorb labor market strength without imminent tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon; strong payrolls alone when FEDFUNDS is already elevated instead raise tightening fears and do not produce the same effect, so the two series must be considered jointly.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with improving labor market
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce 5-day forward SPX hit rate below 50% or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 1-year window, or the single-series PAYEMS-only model performs equally well, negating the need for interaction.
